title: Amend Without Changing Authorship
description: The last commit has a typo in the commit message and is missing one small file. You need to fix both by amending the commit — but the original author information (name and email) must be preserved exactly as it was. The committer can change, but not the author.
objective: The most recent commit must have a corrected message (no typo), must include the missing file `CHANGELOG.md`, and must retain the original author name `Jane Doe` and email `jane@example.com`. The commit count must remain the same.
hints: `git commit --amend --no-edit` amends the commit without changing the message. Add `--author` to override author info.
`git commit --amend --author="Jane Doe <jane@example.com>"` explicitly sets the author on the amended commit.
Stage the missing file with `git add CHANGELOG.md` before amending.
